info.name: Oxxila-backend-api
info.description:
Oxxila e-commerce REST API.

## Structure

- **System** — `GET /` health (no `/api` prefix).
    
- **Authentication** — register/login, `GET /auth/me`, refresh, OAuth (Google/Facebook), forgot/verify/reset password.
    
- **Current User** — getMe, updateMe, avatar, password, active flags, **Profile addresses**, **Browsing history**, **Recommendations**.
    
- **Admin User Management** — admin-only user CRUD + moderation actions.
    
- **Categories & Sub-categories** — Categories, Sub-categories, nested routes, Brands. See `docs/CATEGORIES_API.md`.
    
- **Products** — list/filter (e.g. `isBestSeller`, `isBundle`, `allOffers`, `todayOffers`, `noOffers`, facets), optional-auth detail (`catalog` in response; browsing history when Bearer), admin multipart CRUD + toggle best seller.
    
- **Offers** — public list/upcoming/detail; admin CRUD + delete all (syncs `priceAfterDiscount` / `offerEndsAt` on products).
    
- **Banners** — public active banners; admin multipart create/update/delete.
    
- **Reviews** — nested under products (list, stats, create); flat routes (get/update/delete, like). Updates product `ratingsAverage` / `ratingsQuantity` automatically.
    
- **FAQs** — public list per product; user **ask specialist** (email to `ADMIN_EMAIL`); admin CRUD on `/faqs` + create under product.
    
- **Coupons** — admin CRUD. User apply/remove on **Cart** (`POST /cart/coupon`, `DELETE /cart/coupon`).
    
- **Orders** — COD checkout, card payment sessions (Stripe/Paymob), my-orders, admin list/status/refund. Test UI: `GET /checkout-test.html`.
    
- **Returns** — eligible delivered orders (14-day window), partial returns, proof images, pickup/self-ship, admin workflow through refund (Stripe/Paymob auto, COD manual).
    
- **Webhooks** — Stripe (`checkout.session.completed`, `charge.refunded`); Paymob processed POST + browser redirect GET (HMAC-signed; not for manual testing without valid signatures).
    
- **Shipping (Checkout)** — public countries, governorates, zones, resolve price, carriers.
    
- **Shipping Admin** — countries, governorates, districts, carriers, coverage, settings (`/api/v1/admin/*`).
    
- **Cart** — authenticated shopping cart: add/update/remove lines, apply/remove coupon, clear cart. Line prices sync from product on every read/write.
    

## Tokens

- **Access token** (15m) — returned in the response body and stored in the `token` env var. Sent via `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`.
    
- **Refresh token** (7d) — set by the server as an `httpOnly`, `Secure`, `SameSite=Strict` cookie scoped to `/api/v1/auth`. Postman's Cookie Jar sends it automatically.
    

## Typical flow

Signup/Login → call protected endpoints → when the access token expires, call **Authentication / Signup & Login / Refresh Access Token** → new access token is saved to `{{token}}` → **Logout** revokes the session.

Select the `Oxxila Dev` or `Oxxila Prod` environment before sending requests.

item.name: Admin User Management

request.description: Supports filter (`?role=user`), search (`?keyword=...`), sort (`?sort=-createdAt`), field selection (`?fields=name,email`), and pagination (`?page`, `?limit`).
Creates any user, including admins. Password is hashed by the User model's pre-save hook.
Fetches a single user by Mongo ObjectId. Returns 404 if not found.
Updates arbitrary fields on a user. Password and role changes are stripped — use **Change Specific User Password** for passwords, and a future dedicated endpoint for role changes.
Hard-deletes a user document. Prefer **Deactivate Current User** for user-initiated offboarding.
Reactivates a previously deactivated account. Useful when a user's `deactivateMe` call needs to be undone by support.
Force-sets a user's password (no old-password check). The user's existing sessions become invalid on next refresh because `passwordChangedAt` is bumped.

item.description: Admin-only operations on user accounts. Requires a Bearer token belonging to a user with role `admin`.

item.name: Authentication
Signup & Login

exec: console.log('Saved access token + userId (refresh cookie handled by Postman)');

request.description: Creates a new account. Response body carries the 15-min access token; the 7-day refresh token is set as an httpOnly cookie. Saves `token` + `userId` to the active environment.

exec: console.log('Saved access token + userId (refresh cookie handled by Postman)');

request.description: Validates credentials. Returns a 15-min access token in the response body and sets a 7-day refresh token as an httpOnly cookie scoped to `/api/v1/auth`. Saves `token` + `userId` to the active environment.

exec: console.log('Rotated refresh cookie and saved new access token');

request.description: Reads the refresh token from the httpOnly cookie (Postman attaches it automatically), verifies it against the DB, rotates it (old is revoked, new one issued), and returns a fresh access token. Using a revoked refresh token revokes every session for the user (reuse detection).
Revokes the current refresh token in DB and clears the cookie. Intentionally unauthenticated so an expired access token can't block logout.

`GET /api/v1/auth/me` — returns a minimal public user from the JWT (`publicUser` shape). For the full Mongo profile (`addresses`, `phone`, `avatar`, …) use **Current User → Get Current User** (`GET /api/v1/users/getMe`).

item.description: Create an account, sign in, refresh the access token, or end the session.

item.name: Password Reset

request.description: Sends a password-reset link to the user's email. The link points to `${CLIENT_URL}/reset-password/<token>`. Always responds 200 to avoid leaking which emails are registered.

variable.description: Plain token from the email (same as in POST reset-password path)

request.description: `GET /api/v1/auth/reset-password/:token` — call before showing the new-password form. `400` with `Reset link is invalid or has expired` if bad.

exec: console.log('Saved new access token (all previous sessions revoked)');

variable.description: Plain reset token from the email link

request.description: Validates the token, checks newPassword === confirmPassword, sets the new password, and revokes all refresh sessions. Response is success only — re-login to obtain tokens. (Postman test script is optional: issue tokens on login/refresh only.)

item.description: Forgot-password flow. Both endpoints are unauthenticated.

item.name: OAuth

request.description: Browser-only: redirects to Google OAuth. After consent, Google calls **Google — callback** and the API sets refresh cookie + redirects to `CLIENT_URL` with access token. Not practical to complete inside Postman without following redirects manually.

query.description: Set by Google — do not call manually

request.description: Called by Google after login. Documented for reference; use a browser flow instead of Postman.
Browser-only: redirects to Facebook OAuth. Same pattern as Google.

query.description: Set by Facebook — do not call manually

request.description: Called by Facebook after login. Documented for reference.

item.description: Social login entry points. Complete the flow in a browser; tokens land on `CLIENT_URL`.
Signup, login, session management, OAuth, and password recovery.

item.name: Categories & Sub-categories
Categories

request.description: Admin. **multipart** (`form-data`) — `name` (required), optional `isActive`, optional `image` file. Slug is generated from `name`.
Public. Active categories with `subcategories` (active) populated. Optional `keyword`, `sort`, `fields`.
Public. One active category with subcategories. Set `{{categoryId}}` from Get All.
Admin. **multipart** — at least one of `name`, `isActive`, `image`.

variable.description: Removes this category and all its sub-categories

request.description: Admin. Deletes category and every nested sub-category.

item.description: Top-level `Category` resource at `/api/v1/categories`.

item.name: Sub-categories

request.description: Admin. `POST` is **only** nested (parent in URL). `category` in body is set from `:categoryId` by the server. Slug from `name`.
Public. Flat list of all active sub-categories. Optional `?category=<ObjectId>` to filter in query.
Public. Single sub-category by id (not scoped to a parent in path).
Admin. **Nested** only — `PUT` must match the parent. Optional move to another `category` in body (ObjectId).
Admin. **Nested** only. Deletes one sub-category under the given parent.

item.description: Sub-category CRUD. Create / Update / Delete use nested routes under a category. Reads also available flat.

item.name: Category / Sub-categories

request.description: Admin. Same as **Sub-categories / Create Sub-category** — add a sub-category under a given category.

item.name: Get All Subcategories for Specific Category

request.description: Public. List active sub-categories for one category (path-scoped, not the flat list).

item.description: Nested relationship: `GET` and `POST` under `/api/v1/categories/:categoryId/subcategories`.

Catalog folder layout: **Categories** (root) → **Sub-categories** (full set with correct URL patterns) → **Category / Sub-categories** (parent-scoped) → **Brands**. Public reads: **noauth**; admin: inherit collection Bearer. Env: `{{categoryId}}`, `{{subCategoryId}}`, `{{brandId}}`.

item.name: Brands

formdata.description: Optional — jpg, png, webp (field name: logo)

request.description: Admin. **multipart** (`form-data`) — `name` (required 2–50 chars), optional `description`, optional `isActive`, optional `logo` file. **Slug** is generated from `name` (not accepted in body).
Public. Active brands only. Optional `keyword`, `sort`.
Public. One active brand. Set `{{brandId}}` from **Get All Brands**.
Admin. **multipart** — optional `name`, `description`, `isActive`, `logo`. Slug follows `name` on save hooks where applicable.
Admin. Deletes one brand.

item.description: `Brand` resource at `/api/v1/brands`. Env: `{{brandId}}` (set from list response). Reads are public (`noauth`); writes use inherited Bearer.

item.name: Products

query.description: Products with no active discount (no priceAfterDiscount or offer ended)

request.description: Public listing with ApiFeatures pagination/sort/keyword. Filters: category, subCategory (comma ids), brand, concerns (comma), `isSensitiveSkin`, `isCertified`, `isBestSeller`, `isBundle`, price range, `allOffers`, `todayOffers`, `noOffers`.
Public. If you send **Bearer** (logged-in user), the server appends this product to **Browsing history**.

formdata.description: Repeat field or JSON array per client; at least one id

request.description: **Admin** Bearer. Multipart: required `name`, `price`, `stock`, `category`, `subCategory` (array rules). Optional: `isBestSeller`, `isBundle`, images (max 5), `certificationImage`, `catalog` file (URL saved on `catalog`).
**Admin** Bearer. Partial updates via multipart; new files replace/add per controller logic. Optional booleans: `isBestSeller`, `isBundle`.
**Admin** Bearer. Flips `isBestSeller`.
**Admin** Bearer.

item.description: `/api/v1/products`. Env: `{{productId}}`. Public reads use **noauth**; writes need **admin** Bearer.

item.name: Reviews

request.description: Public. Rating histogram (5→1), counts, percentages, plus `ratingsAverage` and `ratingsQuantity` from the product document.
Public paginated list. Populates `user` (name, avatar). One review per user per product (unique index).

**User or admin** Bearer. `rating` 1–5, `comment` required, `title` optional. Active product only; 400 if you already reviewed this product. Updates product `ratingsAverage` / `ratingsQuantity` via model hook.
Public single review with populated `user` (name, avatar).
**Owner** Bearer only. Partial body allowed (`title`, `comment`, `rating`). Re-runs product rating aggregation on save.
**Owner** or **admin** Bearer. Removes review and recalculates product ratings.
**User** Bearer. Like or unlike; response includes `likesCount` and `liked` boolean.

item.description: Nested: `GET|POST /api/v1/products/:productId/reviews` (+ `.../stats`). Flat: `GET|PUT|DELETE /api/v1/reviews/:id`, `POST .../like`. Set env `{{reviewId}}` from create/list responses; `{{productId}}` from products.

item.name: FAQs

request.description: Public. Active FAQs only (`isActive: true`), newest `createdAt` first.
**User or admin** Bearer. Sends HTML email to `ADMIN_EMAIL` (requires Gmail `EMAIL_USER` / `EMAIL_PASS` on the server). Active product only; 503 if `ADMIN_EMAIL` is unset.
**Admin** Bearer. `question` + `answer` required; optional `isActive` (default true).
**Admin** Bearer. At least one of `question`, `answer`, `isActive`.
**Admin** Bearer.

item.description: Nested: `GET /api/v1/products/:productId/faqs`, `POST .../faqs/ask`, `POST .../faqs` (admin). Flat: `PUT|DELETE /api/v1/faqs/:id`. Env: `{{productId}}`, `{{faqId}}` (from create response).

item.name: Browsing & recommendations

request.description: Last 10 viewed products (populated). History is filled when a logged-in user calls **GET /api/v1/products/:id**.
Empties `browsingHistory` for the current user.
Products from top categories in your history, excluding already-viewed ids; capped at 20.

item.description: Requires Bearer. Uses history populated via authenticated product detail views.

item.name: Offers

request.description: Nearest future active offer (`startDate` > now). Must be registered **before** `GET /:id` in the router.
Active offers where now is between `startDate` and `endDate`. Optional `?todayOffers=true`.
Single offer with populated product card fields.
**Admin** Bearer. ISO8601 dates; `endDate` after `startDate`. Only one active non-expired offer per product. Syncs product `priceAfterDiscount` / `offerEndsAt`.
**Admin** Bearer. Re-syncs affected products when product or discount window changes.
**Admin** Bearer. Clears synced pricing on the linked product.
**Admin** Bearer. `DELETE /` with no body — removes every offer and clears `priceAfterDiscount` / `offerEndsAt` on all affected products.

item.description: `/api/v1/offers`. Public: upcoming, list, get by id. Admin: create, update, delete one, delete all. Env: `{{offerId}}`.

item.name: Banners

request.description: Public list of `isActive` banners, newest `createdAt` first.
**Admin** Bearer. `image` file required. For `linkType` url use `externalUrl`; for product/category use `linkId`.
**Admin** Bearer. Optional new `image`; other fields merged.
**Admin** Bearer.

item.description: `/api/v1/banners`. Env: `{{bannerId}}`. Carousel assets uploaded to Cloudinary folder `oxxila/banners`.

item.name: Current User
Profile addresses

request.description: Returns `data.addresses` — use each entry’s `_id` in orders (e.g. dropdown). Phone for delivery is on the user (`GET getMe` / `phone` field), not on each address.

`POST` with `city` + `address` (required). **Requires** a non-empty `phone` on the user profile (`PATCH updateMe` first) — otherwise `400` with message about adding phone in account settings. Response includes the new subdocument in `data.address` (with `_id`).

variable.description: Subdocument _id from list or from add response

request.description: Send at least one of `city`, `address`. `404` if the id is not in your `addresses` array.
Removes one saved address by `_id`.

item.description: CRUD for embedded `user.addresses`. Requires Bearer token. Add flow depends on `phone` on the user; see **Update Current User**.

request.description: Full Mongo user document: `name`, `email`, `phone`, `avatar`, `addresses`, … (vs **Authentication → Get current user (auth /me)**, which returns a minimal `publicUser` shape). Requires Bearer token.

Sends any subset: `name`, `email`, `phone` (omit keys you are not changing). **Avatar** is not updated here — use **Upload Profile Image**.

- Google-linked users (`googleId` set) **cannot** change email: send `email` in the body with such an account → `400` `Google-linked accounts cannot change their email`.
- Add **`phone` on the profile** before using **Add address** (API requires a profile phone to add an address).

formdata.description: Image file — jpeg, jpg, png, or webp. Max 2 MB.

request.description:
Uploads a new avatar image to Cloudinary and replaces the previous one (old asset is destroyed). Send as `multipart/form-data` with a single `avatar` file field — do NOT set Content-Type manually, Postman adds the correct boundary.

Errors:
- `400` missing file, unsupported format, or larger than 2 MB
- `401` no / invalid access token
Verifies `oldPassword`, then updates to `newPassword`. On success, `passwordChangedAt` is bumped so other sessions fail on next refresh.
Sets `active=false` on the current user. Subsequent auth checks will reject the token with 403.
Sets `active=true` on the current user.

item.description: Self-service endpoints. All require a valid Bearer token. Includes browsing history and recommendations (history is built when you open a product detail with the same token).

item.name: Cart

request.description: **User** Bearer. Returns populated cart with `subtotal`, `discountAmount`, `totalPrice`, and optional `couponCode`. Line `price` is synced from `product.priceAfterDiscount ?? product.price`. Empty cart → `{ items: [], subtotal: 0, discountAmount: 0, totalPrice: 0, couponCode: null }`.
**User** Bearer. Body `{ productId, quantity? }` — `quantity` defaults to `1`. Creates cart or merges quantity on existing line. `404` inactive/missing product; `400` insufficient stock.

variable.description: Cart line subdocument _id from Get/Add Cart

request.description: **User** Bearer. Body `{ quantity }` — updates one line. Same stock/active rules as add. `404` if cart or item missing.
**User** Bearer. Removes one line; response includes remaining `items`, `subtotal`, and `totalPrice`.

**User** Bearer. Body `{ code }` — persists coupon on the cart (`couponCode`, `couponId`, `discountAmount`). Validates expiry, usage cap, per-user reuse, and `minOrderAmount` against cart subtotal. Does **not** increment `usageCount` (that runs at order commit). `400` empty cart or invalid coupon.
**User** Bearer. Clears `couponCode`, `couponId`, and `discountAmount` on the cart. `404` if no cart exists.
**User** Bearer. Deletes the entire cart document for the current user.

item.description: `/api/v1/cart` — all routes require Bearer token. Env: `{{productId}}`, `{{cartItemId}}`, `{{cartId}}`. Test UI: `GET /cart-test.html`.

item.name: Orders
Checkout (user)

request.description: **User** Bearer. Non-empty cart required. Validates stock, coupon, and shipping. Creates order immediately with `paymentMethod: cod`, `paymentStatus: paid`, decrements stock, clears cart, commits coupon usage. `districtId` may be a Mongo id or `"other"` for governorate fallback. Saves `{{orderId}}`.

**User** Bearer. Snapshots cart into a `PaymentSession` (24h TTL). Returns `paymentSessionId`, `provider`, `totalPrice`, `expiresAt`, and `url` (Stripe Checkout) — open `url` in a browser to pay. Order is created on Stripe webhook `checkout.session.completed`. Card payments must not use `POST /orders` with `paymentMethod: card`.

**User** Bearer. Same flow as Stripe but `provider: paymob`. Response includes `paymentSessionId`, `totalPrice`, `expiresAt`, and `iframeUrl` — open the iframe URL in a browser to pay. Fulfillment runs on Paymob processed webhook or redirect callback when `success: true` and HMAC is valid.

**User** Bearer. Poll after redirect from Stripe/Paymob. Returns `status` (`pending` | `processing` | `completed` | `failed` | `expired`), `provider`, `totalPrice`, and populated `order` when completed. Saves `{{orderId}}` when present.
**User** Bearer. Paginated list of the current user's orders (`ApiFeatures`: filter, sort, fields, pagination).
**User** Bearer. Single order owned by the current user. `404` if id missing or not yours.

item.description: Requires Bearer token + non-empty cart (except poll after payment). Use **Shipping (Checkout)** first to obtain `{{governorateId}}` / `{{districtId}}`. Typical card flow: payment-session → pay in browser → poll session status.

item.name: Admin

request.description: **Admin** Bearer. All orders with `user` populated (`name`, `email`, `phone`). Supports filter/sort/pagination.
**Admin** Bearer. Single order with populated user.

**Admin** Bearer. Body `{ orderStatus }` — one of: `pending`, `processing`, `shipped`, `delivered`, `partially_returned`, `returned`, `cancelled`. Setting `delivered` sets `deliveredAt` (starts return window).

**Admin** Bearer. Full gateway + DB refund for paid card orders (`paymentProvider: stripe` or `paymob`, `paymentStatus: paid`). Stripe refunds via Payment Intent; Paymob refunds via transaction id in `paymentReference`. Restores stock; sets `paymentStatus: refunded`, `orderStatus: cancelled`. Response includes `gatewayRefundId` and legacy `stripeRefundId` when provider is Stripe. Idempotent if already refunded. COD → `400`.

item.description: Requires admin Bearer. Env: `{{orderId}}`.
`/api/v1/orders` — checkout and order management. Env: `{{governorateId}}`, `{{districtId}}`, `{{orderId}}`, `{{paymentSessionId}}`. Test UI: `GET /checkout-test.html`.

item.name: Returns
User

request.description: **User** Bearer. Delivered / partially_returned orders within `RETURN_WINDOW_DAYS` (default 14) from `deliveredAt`, with per-line `returnableQuantity` (accounts for pending/approved returns).

formdata.description: JSON array — orderItemId from eligible-orders response
damaged_item | wrong_product | allergic_reaction | expired_product | changed_mind | other

request.description: **User** Bearer. `multipart/form-data`. Order must be `delivered` within return window. Saves `{{returnId}}`.
**User** Bearer. JSON body without proof images — use for `changed_mind`, `expired_product`, `other`. For damaged/wrong/allergic use multipart with `proofImages`.
**User** Bearer. Paginated return history.

item.name: Get My Return Request By ID

request.description: **User** Bearer. Single return with populated order.

item.description: Customer return flow. Mark order **delivered** first (admin) to set `deliveredAt` and start the 14-day window.

item.name: Admin

request.description: **Admin** Bearer. Review queue with user + order populated.
**Admin** Bearer. Full detail including `proofImages`.
**Admin** Workflow: `pending` → `approved` → `picked_up` → `received` → `refunded` (or `pending`/`approved` → `rejected`).
**Admin** `approved` → `picked_up`.
**Admin** `picked_up` → `received` after warehouse inspection.
**Admin** `received` → `refunded`. Card: Stripe/Paymob partial refund + restock. COD: no gateway — set `manualRefundNote` (bank/wallet/Vodafone Cash) and restock.
**Admin** `pending` or `approved` → `rejected`.

item.description: Admin review and refund pipeline. Env: `{{returnId}}`, `{{orderId}}`.
`/api/v1/returns` — return window from `deliveredAt` (`RETURN_WINDOW_DAYS`, default 14). Admin must set order to **delivered** to start the clock.

item.name: Coupons

request.description: **Admin** Bearer. Paginated list via `ApiFeatures` (`filter`, `sort`, `paginate`).
**Admin** Bearer. `code` uppercased in controller. `discountType`: `percentage` | `fixed`. Optional `maxUsage`, `expiresAt`, `minOrderAmount`, `isActive`.
**Admin** Bearer.
**Admin** Bearer. Partial update — send only fields to change.
**Admin** Bearer.

item.description: `/api/v1/coupons` — admin CRUD only. User-facing coupon apply/remove lives under **Cart** (`POST /cart/coupon`, `DELETE /cart/coupon`). Env: `{{couponId}}`.

item.name: Shipping (Checkout)

request.description: Public. Active countries only — `name`, `code`, `flag`, `currency`.
Public. Active governorates with `name`, `shippingPrice`.
Public. If districts exist → `{ hasDistricts: true, districts[], other: { label, shippingPrice } }`. Else → `{ hasDistricts: false, shippingPrice }`.

query.description: Optional — omit or use `other` for governorate fallback

request.description: Public. `governorateId` required. `districtId` optional — missing/`other`/uncovered → governorate fallback price (`isOther: true`).
Public. Carriers with active coverage for the governorate, filtered by `ShippingSettings` type toggles (`api`, `known`, `internal`).

item.description: `/api/v1/shipping` — public checkout reads. Env: `{{countryId}}`, `{{governorateId}}`, `{{districtId}}`.

item.name: Shipping Admin
Countries

request.description: **Admin** Bearer. All countries (including inactive).
**Admin** Bearer. `code` must be 2 chars (uppercased on save). `400` if code already exists.
**Admin** Bearer.
**Admin** Bearer. Cascades delete to all governorates and districts.
**Admin** Bearer. All governorates for country (including inactive).

item.description: `/api/v1/admin/countries`

item.name: Governorates

request.description: **Admin** Bearer. Body `{ country, name, shippingPrice }`. `shippingPrice` is fallback when no districts or when checkout selects Other.
**Admin** Bearer.
**Admin** Bearer. Deletes governorate and all its districts.
**Admin** Bearer. All districts (including `isCovered: false`).

item.description: `/api/v1/admin/governorates`

item.name: Districts

request.description: **Admin** Bearer. Body `{ governorate, name, shippingPrice }`. Default `isCovered: true`.
**Admin** Bearer. Set `isCovered: false` to hide from public zone listing.
**Admin** Bearer.

item.description: `/api/v1/admin/districts`

item.name: Carriers

request.description: **Admin** Bearer. Each carrier includes a `coverage` array of governorate names.
**Admin** Bearer. Create `known` or `internal` carriers. `type: api` carriers (Bosta/Mylerz) are seeded by system — prefer not creating via admin.
**Admin** Bearer. `type` and `apiProvider` cannot be changed.
**Admin** Bearer. Deletes carrier and all coverage rows.
**Admin** Bearer. Coverage rows with populated governorate `name`.
**Admin** Bearer. Replaces all coverage — body `{ governorateIds: [] }`. Empty array clears coverage.

item.description: `/api/v1/admin/carriers` + coverage. Env: `{{carrierId}}`.

item.name: Shipping Settings

request.description: **Admin** Bearer. Toggles `api`, `known`, `internal` carrier visibility at checkout. Auto-creates defaults if missing.
**Admin** Bearer. Send any subset of `api`, `known`, `internal` booleans.

item.description: `/api/v1/admin/shipping-settings` — global carrier type toggles.
`/api/v1/admin/*` — all routes require admin Bearer. Ops UI: `GET /shipping-admin.html`. Env: `{{countryId}}`, `{{governorateId}}`, `{{districtId}}`, `{{carrierId}}`.

item.name: Webhooks

header.description: Required — from Stripe CLI: stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/api/v1/webhooks/stripe

request.description: Public. Raw JSON body + valid `stripe-signature` (server uses `express.raw`). Handles `checkout.session.completed` (fulfills `PaymentSession` → creates order) and `charge.refunded` (syncs refund to DB). **Use Stripe CLI** to forward real signed events — manual Postman calls fail signature verification unless you copy a real payload + header from the CLI.

Public. Paymob **processed** server callback (POST). `type` must be `TRANSACTION`; `obj.success: true` fulfills the payment session. `hmac` must match server-side HMAC of `obj` fields — use Paymob dashboard test callbacks or compute HMAC with `PAYMOB_HMAC_SECRET`. Failed payments set session `failed` when `merchant_order_id` is present.

query.description: Required — HMAC-SHA512 over Paymob field order (see PAYMOB_HMAC_SECRET)

request.description: Public. Paymob **response** redirect after checkout (GET). Verifies `hmac`, fulfills the payment session when `success=true`, then redirects to `PAYMOB_RETURN_URL` (or checkout test page) with `?payment=completed|failed&paymentSessionId=…`. Normally hit by the browser after Paymob payment — not typical Postman testing unless you copy a real redirect URL from Paymob.

item.description: Provider callbacks — not for normal API testing without valid signatures. Env: `{{paymentSessionId}}`.

item.name: System

request.description: No `/api` prefix. Returns `{ success, message: 'Oxxila API is up and running' }` when the server is healthy.

item.description: Root-level routes outside `/api/v1`.
